questions.statement: Which wildlife sanctuaries is a tri-state protected area in northern India, Which is also famous for the Critically Endangered gharial and Dolphin?
Which region in India has the highest concentration of Iron and Steel Industry?
The Governor of a State holds his office during the pleasure of the :
questions.explanation:
The correct answer is - President

Key Points

  • Governor's Tenure
    • The Governor of a state holds office during the pleasure of the President.
    • This provision is outlined in Article 156 of the Indian Constitution.
    • The Governor serves as the representative of the President in the state and performs duties as per constitutional guidelines.
  • Executive Authority
    • The President has the authority to appoint, dismiss, or require the resignation of a Governor without providing reasons.
    • The Governor does not serve a fixed term but continues in office until the President decides otherwise.

Additional Information

  • Appointment of Governor
    • The Governor is appointed by the President under Article 155 of the Constitution.
    • Eligibility for appointment includes:
      • Being a citizen of India.
      • Being at least 35 years old.
  • Constitutional Role
    • The Governor acts as the constitutional head of the state, with executive powers vested in him/her.
    • However, the Governor performs most functions on the advice of the Council of Ministers, headed by the Chief Minister.
  • Removal of Governor
    • The Constitution does not specify the grounds for removal, making the Governor's position subject to the discretion of the President.
    • The Supreme Court has clarified that the Governor cannot be removed on arbitrary grounds unrelated to constitutional propriety.

The Agriculture Infrastructure Fund (AIF) provides support for?
questions.explanation:
The correct answer is Agri-logistics and cold chains.

Key Points

  • The Agriculture Infrastructure Fund (AIF) is a financing facility launched by the Government of India in 2020 to strengthen agricultural infrastructure across the country.
  • It provides medium to long-term debt financing for investment in viable projects for post-harvest management infrastructure and community farming assets.
  • Key projects funded under the AIF include cold storage, warehousing, sorting & grading units, packhouses, ripening chambers, and other agri-logistics infrastructure.
  • Through this initiative, farmers and Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs) can access loans with interest subvention and credit guarantee support.
  • The fund also aims to reduce post-harvest losses and improve the efficiency of agricultural supply chains, ensuring better price realization for farmers.

Additional Information

  • Free Fertilizers
    • The government of India provides subsidies on fertilizers to ensure affordability for farmers, but there is no provision for free fertilizers under the AIF.
    • Subsidized fertilizers such as urea, DAP, and MOP are made available through schemes like the Nutrient Based Subsidy (NBS) scheme.
    • The subsidy is directly transferred to fertilizer companies, which reduces the retail price for farmers.
  • Cash Transfers
    • Cash transfers are direct benefit transfers (DBT) under schemes like PM-KISAN, where eligible farmers receive Rs. 6,000 annually in three installments.
    • The objective of cash transfers is to provide income support to farmers for agricultural and allied activities.
    • However, AIF does not involve cash transfers and focuses on infrastructure development.
  • MGNREGA Work
    • The Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA) provides wage employment for rural households through unskilled manual work.
    • It aims to enhance livelihood security and create durable assets in rural areas.
    • While MGNREGA contributes to rural development, it is unrelated to the objectives of AIF, which targets agricultural infrastructure.
